title: SO 2387(E) 4 October 2012 regarding Privileges and Immunities to Sub Regional Office for South and South West Asia of the UN Economic and Social Commission for Asia and the Pacific
description: SO 2387(E) 4 October 2012 regarding Privileges and Immunities to Sub Regional Office for South and South West Asia of the UN Economic and Social Commission for Asia and the Pacific
text: . ~'. -. 2 THE GAZETTE OF INDIA: EXTRAORDINARY [PART IJ--Soc. 3(ii)] MINISTRY OF EXTERNAL AFFAIRS / (Protocol Division) NOI1F1CATION New Delhi, the 4th October, 2012 S. O. 2387(E).-In exercise of the powers conferred by Section 3 of the United Nations (Privileges and Imrnunities) Act, 1947 (46 of 1947), the Central Govenunent hereby declares that the provisions of Articles T, 11, 111and Article V [except provisions under clause (e) of Section 18 and Section 19], Article VI, Sections 24, 25 and 26 of Article VII and Section 29 of Article VI [] of the Schedule to the said Act shall apply, mutatis mutandis, to the "Sub-Regional Office for South and South- West Asia ofthe United Nations Economic and Social Commission for Asia andthe Pacific", established in New Delhi by the agreement entere.d into between the Government oflndiaand the United Nations on J 3th day of March, 2012, and to its officials and experts on mission, subject to the following modifications, namely :- ' MODlFlCATIONS In the Articles so applied :---.::- (A) for the reference to the expressions "United Nations" and "premises", the expressions "Sub-Regional Office for South and South-West Asia ofthe United Nations Economic and Social Commission for Asia and the Pacific" and "Office premises" shall respectively be substituted; (B) clauses (d), (t) and (g) of Section J 8 or Article V ofthe Schedule shall also apply to the experts on mission. (please see the Annexure). [F. No. 0-11I451116(15)196] RUCHIRA KAMBOJ, JL Secy. and ChiefofProtocol ANNEXURE , Following sections of Article 18 shall apply to the experts on mission: (d) be immune, together with their spouses and relatives dependent on them, from immigration restrictions and alien registration; (t) be given, together with their spouses and relatives dependent on them, the same repatriation facilities in time ofintemational crisis as diplomatic envoys; (g) have the right to import free of duty their fumiture and effects at the time of first taking up their post in the .. country in question.
